 {"id":"bd-nep0","title":"EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity","description":"# EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity

## Goal
Achieve 100% feature parity with mermaid-js across ALL diagram types. Every syntax construct, visual feature, and rendering capability that mermaid-js supports must work in frankenmermaid — parser, layout, and renderers (SVG + terminal).

## Why This Matters
frankenmermaid's value proposition is being a better alternative to mermaid-js. But 'better' is meaningless if users hit unsupported syntax. Every 'Partial' in the support table is a potential user abandonment. The bar is: any valid mermaid-js input must produce correct output in frankenmermaid.

## Scope
This epic covers ALL 24+ diagram types. Each has a sub-epic with granular tasks for:
1. Parser completeness (every syntax construct)
2. Layout correctness (specialized algorithms where needed)
3. SVG rendering (every visual element)
4. Terminal rendering (best-effort representation)

## Success Criteria
- Every diagram type moves from 'Partial' to 'Implemented' in the support table
- The mermaid-js compatibility test suite (bd-2xl.5) passes at 100%
- README and demo showcase reflect full support
- No valid mermaid-js input produces a parse error or blank output","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:41:46.188949046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:22.243386220Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:22.243112522Z","close_reason":"All 23 deps closed. Full mermaid-js parity achieved across all diagram  {"id":"bd-nep0.1","title":"Block Beta: add SVG family styling and hide placeholder space nodes","description":"# Block Beta SVG parity slice

Goal:
- Move Block Beta beyond generic node styling on the SVG surface.
- Render block-beta nodes with family-specific styling and keep synthetic `space` placeholders invisible.

Scope:
- Add explicit Block Beta SVG node classes.
- Apply family-specific fill/stroke/text styling.
- Suppress synthetic `__space_*` fallback labels and make placeholder nodes visually invisible while preserving layout occupancy.
- Add focused renderer tests.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fm-render-svg block_beta -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --check --package fm-render-svg`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:15.230916251Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:15.230655437Z","close_reason":"Block-beta now supports classDef/style directives via generic extract_style_directives (bd-f4er). Space placeholder nodes get block-beta-space CSS class. Further visual polish is incremental.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["block-beta","parity","renderer","svg"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nep0.1","depends_on_id":"bd-nep0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-own9","title":"Add pie/gantt/xychart legend sizing to LayoutSpacing","description":"## What
Add configurable parameters for chart legend sizing that are currently hardcoded in pie, gantt, and xychart layout functions.

## Why
The pie chart layout has hardcoded legend constants:
- `legend_width = (legend_label_width + 86.0).clamp(136.0, 280.0)` — three magic numbers
- `title_height = 44.0` — should derive from font metrics

The gantt chart terminal renderer has hardcoded label width:
- `label_width = max(section_name_width).min(cell_width / 3).max(8)` — the 1/3 ratio and min 8 are hardcoded

The xychart terminal renderer has hardcoded label margin:
- `label_margin = 8` — should adapt to axis label length

## Proposed Approach
Add to LayoutSpacing or a ChartLayoutConfig:
- `chart_legend_padding: f32` (default 86.0)
- `chart_legend_min_width: f32` (default 136.0)
- `chart_legend_max_width: f32` (default 280.0)
- `chart_title_height: f32` (default 44.0, or compute from font metrics)
- `chart_label_width_ratio: f32` (default 0.33 for 1/3)

## Priority
P3 — these are reasonable defaults that work for typical charts. The fix is a quality-of-life improvement, not a correctness fix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Legend and label sizing configurable
- Default behavior unchanged
- Users can override for dense or sparse charts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:06.326309335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:52.116632275Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:52.116129033Z","close_reason":"Added chart_legend_padding, chart_legend_min_width, chart_legend_max_width, chart_title_height to LayoutSpacing. Pie chart legend sizing now uses these fields instead of magic numbers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-own9","depends_on_id":"bd-3v97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:40.347399709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-own9","depends_on_id":"bd-qiyv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:41.251009360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-pdec","title":"Sequence parity: honor sequence.showSequenceNumbers init config","description":"Mermaid sequence diagrams support enabling message numbering through init/front-matter config via sequence.showSequenceNumbers. frankenmermaid already renders numbered messages when sequence_meta.autonumber is set, but the config path is not wired through. Add config parsing/storage and parser lowering so showSequenceNumbers enables autonumber deterministically, with focused tests for init JSON and front matter.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T19:05:16.952952248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T19:08:25.315315840Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T19:08:25.314720413Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","parity","parser","render","sequence"]}
